domain: optical caustic networks (singular focusing curves of light through water surface or refractive media; Berry-Thom catastrophe classification provides 7 elementary stable caustic types: fold, cusp, swallowtail, butterfly, hyperbolic-umbilic, elliptic-umbilic, parabolic-umbilic; rogue-wave formation is caustic phenomenon in ocean wave field)
specific_mechanism: Light rays through water surface (or other refractive media) converge into singular curves of high intensity = caustics. Berry-Thom catastrophe theory proves any STABLE singular locus belongs to one of 7 elementary classes characterised by their unfolding structure. Higher-order catastrophes are unstable. The catastrophe TYPE characterises the local structure of the singularity independent of physical scale.
llm_application: Catastrophe-class fingerprinting of attention spikes: for each attention spike (a position where attention amplitude exceeds a threshold), measure the local shape around the spike (gradient field, Hessian, third-order structure tensor). Project this shape into the 7-elementary-catastrophe Thom-Berry classification. The catastrophe-class label is a structural fingerprint. Audit signal: jailbreak-induced or hallucination-onset attention spikes may exhibit different catastrophe-class distribution than benign spikes (e.g., more swallowtail/butterfly types). Detection pipeline: classify-spikes → flag-anomalous-class-distribution → trigger downstream check. Distinct from amplitude-based detection: classifies STRUCTURE, not magnitude.
novelty_claim: Star Attention (2411.17116) and Controlling Logits (2511.21377) address attention spike AMPLITUDE. Logit Suppression Vulnerability (2405.13068) is an attack. None propose Berry-Thom catastrophe-class structural fingerprinting of attention spikes for audit. The 7-class topology audit is not in surveyed prior art.
